rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Verify capabilities BEFORE asserting, not after.
Known correct Claude Code facts (easy to get wrong).
Sessions are the unit of work — 'start a new session in X,' not 'open Claude Code in X.'
Training platform = Claude Code (CLI + Desktop) + Cowork.
Pseudo-URI + kill-switch for public/private content split.

rules_evaluated.evidence: line:49: '[Mastering Claude Code in 30 minutes](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=6eBSHbLKuN0) (Boris Cherny, creator of Claude Code, ~30 min)' — URL present, source-type label absent. Should be [practitioner direct].
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add '[practitioner direct]' label after the URL reference for the Cherny video on line 49.

rules_evaluated.evidence: line:51: 'Cherny calls parallel worktrees his single biggest productivity unlock' — specific attributed claim, no explicit URL on this sentence (URL lives two sentences above in the same paragraph), no source-type label.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: This claim's source is implicit (the video link at line 49). Either repeat the URL inline or make the attribution explicit enough that the video link carries it. Add source-type label [practitioner direct].

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Source-verification maintainer block on any student-facing file with practitioner citations.
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:49: student-facing Cherny citation with named practitioner + specific URL present. Maintainer block (below <!-- maintainer -->) has no '**Source verification — MUST DO before first cohort:**' section. Rule 11 fires when named practitioner + specific URL appears in student-facing body.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add source-verification section to maintainer block: list the Cherny video URL to verify; note the freshness exception decision; name fallback framing if video becomes unavailable.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Installed Claude Code Skills invoke by name, never by path.
Artifact-realism check — every named tool, skill, connector, file, or path must exist and be available.
Verification machinery needs a smoke-test before being trusted.

platform_claims_checked.claim: Use /context to see how much your context window has filled up.
platform_claims_checked.notes: Live-test (2026-04-27, compounded/2026-04-26-platform-live-test-beats-docs-for-behavior-claims.md Failure 3) confirmed /context shows context budget by category. 'How much your context window has filled up' is accurate. Old misframing ('what files Claude read') was corrected; current language is clean.

platform_claims_checked.claim: /context shows how much of your window has filled up. The slice of the repo Claude didn't load is the bounded-window reality.
platform_claims_checked.notes: First sentence accurate. Second sentence is a logical inference (bounded window means some files not loaded), not a claim that /context shows the file list. orient-and-introspect.md line 41 correctly explains 'breakdown by category (system prompt, messages, memory, skills)'. Module file does not misrepresent the command output.

platform_claims_checked.claim: ./CLAUDE.local.md sits at the top of the next session, waiting to be read (alongside team ./CLAUDE.md if this repo has one; both concatenate into context).
platform_claims_checked.notes: CLAUDE.local.md is in the auto-load tier per reference_claude_code_capabilities.md and check_platform_and_boundaries.md §7a. 'Concatenate into context' is correct — both files merge into context window at session start.

platform_claims_checked.claim: Boris Cherny, creator of Claude Code
platform_claims_checked.notes: Confirmed in memory (check_platform_and_boundaries.md §9 cites Cherny as at Anthropic; voice-spec and project memory treat him as creator). Factually accurate.

platform_claims_checked.claim: Delivery architecture: content folder unzipped by student at prework; all compounding artifacts land in the student's real repo.
platform_claims_checked.notes: AE101 uses student's existing repo — no training-dir state. Consistent with check_pedagogy.md §35 (engineer tier = multi-layer CLAUDE.md) and AE101 strategy.

citations_checked.claim: Boris Cherny Mastering Claude Code in 30 minutes YouTube video
citations_checked.notes: URL alive (HTTP 200). Should be labeled [practitioner direct] — Cherny's own video on his own practice. Missing label is a rule-1 violation.

citations_checked.claim: Cherny calls parallel worktrees his single biggest productivity unlock
citations_checked.notes: Specific attributed claim. Source is the video at line 49 (same paragraph). No explicit URL or label on this sentence. Non-blocking because URL is present in the same paragraph, but technically a separate claim without its own cite anchor.
Maintainer-block only (below <!-- maintainer -->). Not student-facing body; verification debt captured here for pre-cohort pass. TODO, not blocking.
Maintainer-block only. Attribution deferred (frame named at M4 by design). TODO, not blocking.
